title: Isolate dependencies per project, not globally
description: Install dependencies into a project-scoped environment (virtualenv, local node_modules, container) so projects do not share or clobber each other's versions.
body: Use when installing dependencies for local development. Do not install project libraries globally, and avoid mutating a shared system environment that other projects rely on. Actions: use a project-local environment and a committed lockfile so installs are deterministic, install from the lockfile rather than loose ranges, and inspect that a clean install reproduces the same versions. Validation signals include a lockfile-driven install producing identical dependency versions across machines and no reliance on globally-installed packages. Risk boundary: a globally-installed dependency that one project mutates can silently break another; flag global installs and prefer isolation, escalating if a tool genuinely must be global.
